mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.8%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of calendar months where UNRATE drops below 4.8%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling housing starts signals a structural shift in capital allocation toward commercial investment, which historically precedes positive corporate earnings surprises and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO/HOUST signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 0.75%, rising commercial and industrial credit demand signals robust economic activity that temporarily overrides rate hike headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.75% and RSAFS rises month-over-month in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation above 0.2% monthly signals overheating demand, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy aggressively, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive despite sustained CPIAUCSL monthly increases exceeding 0.2% in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls coupled with a declining unemployment rate indicates rapid labor market tightening that historically precedes short-term earnings surprises, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal produces negative or flat 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 103.5, manufacturing capacity utilization peaks, leading to supply bottlenecks that squeeze corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of months where INDPRO remains above 103.5.
